scenario_results.reason: Normal analytics work stayed inside architecture-and-engineering-specific analysis support, tied metrics to named phase, model, drawing, review, and permit records, and avoided adjacent authority claims.
Disputed phase-delivery and fee-burn logic was treated as a publication blocker and escalated instead of being guessed.
Stale drawing, model-check, and review files were surfaced as confidence-breaking evidence gaps rather than silently absorbed into the analysis.
Conflicting PSA or ERP, CDE, BIM, QA or QC, and permit systems were preserved with a reconciliation posture and no false definitive metric was produced.
Unsupported causal and forecast language was refused and replaced with evidence-bounded analysis wording.
Disclosure-ready output was blocked when open review issues, permit posture, or seal context did not support the requested claim.
The lane refused generic consulting framing and required phase, discipline, BIM, CAD, permit, and licensure context.
Permit, code, scope-approval, and sign-and-seal authority stayed outside the lane, with only analytics-safe context preserved.
Missing jurisdiction, discipline-of-record, and life-safety facts correctly changed the analytical posture and blocked confident reporting.
Public owner guidance, tenant contract rules, and internal phase definitions were preserved as a conflict instead of collapsed into a single unsupported readiness claim.
Tenant-specific stage and fee rules were recorded explicitly while preserving the public baseline and escalation posture.
Realistic architecture-and-engineering terminology and artifact shapes produced an industry-specific analysis package rather than generic BI output.

notes: All 12 scenarios are represented in evaluation/functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and cross-industry abilities are mapped to one or more scenarios.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard analytics-reporting-and-disclosure threshold of 0.92.
Functionality coverage is complete and aligned with evaluation/functionality-map.json.
Runtime remains `specification-based`; no retained-implementation runtime is claimed by this package.
